title: Alsinopsis douglasii
intro:
There is not enough information on Alsinopsis douglasii in the available data. However, I can provide information on a plant with a similar name, Douglasia. 

Douglasia is a plant genus in the heath family (Ericaceae) and is commonly known as the Douglas's azalea or the Douglas's primrose. It is a flowering plant with several species found in the mountainous regions of North America.

Douglasia is used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating digestive issues, anxiety, and as an anti-inflammatory agent. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.

The bioactive constituents of Douglasia include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

Douglasia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures. However, please consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Alsinopsis douglasii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This family is commonly known as the Jacob's ladder or Greek valerian family. The genus Alsinopsis is a small group of plants closely related to Polemonium and Yermo. Alsinopsis douglasii is native to western North America and is often found in dry to moist areas, typically in full sun to partial shade. The classification of Alsinopsis douglasii reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Polemoniaceae family.
